description: Full-route open-transit city bundles for H2O+ and CFCMT single-city/cross-city validation.

singapore_lta_all.demand_source: LTA DataMall PV/ODBus 202604, WEEKDAY monthly totals divided to typical weekday
singapore_lta_all.traffic_source: LTA TrafficSpeedBands snapshot nearest-matched to stop segments

austin_capmetro_all.demand_source: GTFS schedule headway proxy OD; no public no-key stop-level ridership found

halifax_transit_all.demand_source: Halifax route-level half-hour APC apportioned across GTFS route-direction-patterns

mbta_all.demand_source: MBTA Fall 2025 stop-level boardings/alightings apportioned across GTFS route-direction-patterns

single_city_validation.protocol: Use full city bundles. Dynamic smoke may sample line-envs for very large bundles, but training/evaluation inputs remain full-route manifests.

paper_hypotheses.h1: Single-city validation should be competitive for both H2O+ and CFCMT when the simulator is calibrated or data-derived.
paper_hypotheses.h2: Under cross-city transfer and uncalibrated simulation, CFCMT should outperform H2O+ because mechanism-level residual/trust adaptation reduces city-specific calibration dependence.
paper_hypotheses.h3: CFCMT with uncalibrated simulation should approach or exceed H2O+ using stronger manual calibration.
